title: Fix hardcoded /Users/lesa fallback path in bridge core.ts
body:
## Problem

`src/bridge/core.ts` line 13 has:
```ts
const HOME = process.env.HOME || "/Users/lesa";
```

This leaks a developer path and will break for any other user where `$HOME` is unset.

## Fix

Replace with `os.homedir()`:
```ts
import { homedir } from 'node:os';
const HOME = process.env.HOME || homedir();
```

## Effort
5 minutes

title: Add test infrastructure using node:test
body:
## Problem

Zero test files in the repo. Core modules (`lib/deploy.mjs`, `lib/state.mjs`, `lib/safe.mjs`) have no automated tests.

## Proposal

Use Node's built-in `node:test` runner (zero external deps, aligns with project philosophy).

### New files
- `test/lib/safe.test.mjs` — trash-instead-of-delete safety net (test first)
- `test/lib/deploy.test.mjs` — deployment engine unit tests
- `test/lib/state.test.mjs` — state reconciliation tests
- `test/bridge/core.test.ts` — config resolution tests

### package.json
```json
"scripts": {
  "test": "node --test test/**/*.test.mjs",
  "test:bridge": "npx tsx --test test/bridge/*.test.ts"
}
```

## Priority test targets (by risk)
1. `lib/safe.mjs` — safety net should itself be tested
2. `lib/deploy.mjs` — 1000+ lines of critical deployment logic
3. `lib/state.mjs` — state reconciliation bugs could cause data loss
4. `src/bridge/core.ts` — config resolution, API key lookup

## Effort
2-4 hours for initial coverage

title: Add GitHub Actions CI pipeline
body:
## Problem

No CI/CD. Build breakage and regressions are only caught manually.

## Proposal

Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:

```yaml
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build:bridge
      - run: npm test
```

Expand later with lint check (`fmt:check`) once Prettier is added.

## Effort
30 minutes

title: Break bin/ldm.js into per-command modules
body:
## Problem

`bin/ldm.js` is 2,600+ lines handling 9+ commands (`init`, `install`, `doctor`, `status`, `sessions`, `msg`, `updates`, `worktree`). Hard to navigate, review, or test in isolation.

## Proposal

Extract each command into its own module:

```
bin/
  ldm.js              (slim dispatcher — parse args, route to command)
  commands/
    init.mjs
    install.mjs
    doctor.mjs
    status.mjs
    sessions.mjs
    msg.mjs
    updates.mjs
    worktree.mjs
```

Each command exports a single `run(args, opts)` function. Shared helpers (console formatting, lockfile, registry) move to `lib/cli-helpers.mjs`.

Can be done incrementally — one command per PR.

## Effort
3-5 hours total (incremental)

title: Replace silent catch blocks with debug logging
body:
## Problem

Multiple `catch {}` blocks suppress errors entirely with no logging:
- `lib/deploy.mjs` (several locations)
- `lib/state.mjs`
- `bin/ldm.js`

This makes debugging failures extremely difficult.

## Proposal

Add a lightweight debug logger:
```javascript
// lib/log.mjs
const DEBUG = process.env.LDM_DEBUG === '1';
export function debug(context, msg, err) {
  if (DEBUG) console.error(`[ldm:${context}] ${msg}`, err?.message || '');
}
```

Replace `catch {}` with `catch (err) { debug('deploy', 'optional step failed', err); }`.

Users opt-in with `LDM_DEBUG=1 ldm install`.

## Effort
1-2 hours

title: Add Prettier config for consistent formatting
body:
## Problem

No linting or formatting config. Style drift is likely with multiple contributors (including AI agents).

## Proposal

Minimal Prettier config (no heavy ESLint — aligns with zero-deps philosophy):

```json
{
  "singleQuote": true,
  "trailingComma": "all",
  "printWidth": 100
}
```

```json
"scripts": {
  "fmt": "npx prettier --write 'src/**/*.{ts,mjs}' 'lib/**/*.mjs' 'bin/**/*.js'",
  "fmt:check": "npx prettier --check 'src/**/*.{ts,mjs}' 'lib/**/*.mjs' 'bin/**/*.js'"
}
```

Add `fmt:check` to CI pipeline. Do one initial format commit, then enforce going forward.

## Effort
1 hour

title: Replace shell rm -rf with fs.rmSync in deploy.mjs
body:
## Problem

`lib/deploy.mjs` line ~709 shells out for `rm -rf` via `execSync`. This has a theoretical shell injection surface and isn't cross-platform.

## Fix

Replace with:
```javascript
fs.rmSync(path, { recursive: true, force: true });
```

Node's built-in `fs.rmSync` is safer (no shell) and cross-platform.

## Effort
15 minutes

title: Add engines field to package.json for Node 18+ requirement
body:
## Problem

`SKILL.md` documents Node 18+ requirement but `package.json` doesn't enforce it.

## Fix

Add to `package.json`:
```json
"engines": {
  "node": ">=18"
}
```

This gives users a clear error on older Node versions instead of cryptic ESM failures.

## Effort
5 minutes

title: Remove dist/ from git tracking
body:
## Problem

`dist/bridge/` contains compiled TypeScript output checked into git. This creates noisy diffs and risks stale builds.

## Fix

1. Add `dist/` to `.gitignore`
2. Add `"prepublishOnly": "npm run build:bridge"` to package.json scripts
3. Remove from tracking: `git rm -r --cached dist/`

**Note:** Only do this if npm publish workflow can run a build step. The `prepublishOnly` hook handles manual publishes. If publishing from CI, add the build step there.

## Effort
15 minutes
